title: Embattled Superintendent of Los Angeles School District Resigns
description: Alberto Carvalho resigned Sunday, months after the F.B.I. raided his home and office. He came to Los Angeles from Miami with a reputation for raising student test scores but also made missteps.
author: Shawn Hubler and Dana Goldstein

title: ‘The world knows we exist now’: Cape Verde fans lure neutral spectators to their side.
description: Dancing exuberantly in the bleachers and teaching the crowd Cape Verde chants.
author: Patricia Mazzei

title: Tornadoes Reported in Southern Illinois Leave Trail of Damage
description: By Sunday night, there had already been at least 13 reports of tornadic activity or damage, a meteorologist said.
author: Emmett Lindner

title: Hantavirus Quarantine Ends for 18 Americans Exposed on a Cruise Ship
description: No cases associated with the outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship have been confirmed in the U.S., health officials said.
author: Maia Spoto

title: Boyle Heights Warehouse Fire Spreads Smoke Across Los Angeles
description: Firefighters say they are making progress against the blaze, which began Wednesday at a cold-storage facility. But smoke continued to blow across the region, and the authorities reiterated air-quality warnings.
author: Maia Spoto

title: Tesla Driver Using Autopilot Crashes Into Home in Texas and Kills a Woman, Officials Say
description: The driver told investigators he was using the vehicle’s automated-driver system when the vehicle left the roadway and crashed into a house in Harris County, Texas.
author: Johnny Diaz

title: F.A.A. Investigates Near Miss Between Planes at Boston’s Logan Airport
description: A Delta Air Lines flight aborted its landing on Saturday to avoid another plane that was taking off from an intersecting runway, the agency said.
author: Yan Zhuang

title: Protect Every Animal From Cruelty? Not in 2026, Oregon Democrats Say
description: A possible referendum in Oregon on animal rights would end fishing, hunting, even pest control, just when Democrats are trying really hard not to be seen as “weirdos again.”
author: Anna Griffin

title: F.A.A. Investigates Near Miss Between Planes at Boston’s Logan Airport
description: A Delta Air Lines flight aborted its landing to avoid another plane that was taking off from an intersecting runway, the agency said.
author: Yan Zhuang

title: How Trump’s Fragile Agreement With Iran Is Shaping the Midterms
description: Democrats say the president started an economically painful war that resulted in nothing positive. Republicans are more divided, even as they show some signs of relief at falling gas prices.
author: Tim Balk and Bayliss Wagner

title: Neither the War Nor Trump’s Deal Terminated the Main Threats in Iran, Analysts Say
description: Neither the war nor the agreement terminated the main threats emanating from Iran, many analysts said.
author: Neil MacFarquhar

title: World Cup Visitors in Miami Encounter Beaches Thick With Smelly Seaweed
description: Carpets of sargassum now regularly invade South Florida’s coastline in June. The timing is unfortunate for visiting soccer fans hoping to sunbathe and swim.
author: David Ovalle

title: A Diocese Tries to Protect Its 29-Foot Jesus From Trump’s Border Wall
description: In a battle of symbols, the Catholics of Las Cruces, N.M., argue that religious freedom should stop the wall from scarring a mountain that has attracted pilgrims for nearly a century.
author: Reis Thebault and Paul Ratje

title: Trump Administration Shuttered a Criminal Probe Into Fraudster’s Clemency
description: Federal prosecutors had been examining the circumstances behind the commutation of David Gentile’s sentence. He was aided by a Catholic priest friendly with the president.
author: Kenneth P. Vogel, Nicole Hong and William K. Rashbaum

title: Wildfire in Central Utah Prompts Evacuation Order for Hundreds of People
description: The Iron fire had burned more than 13,000 acres by Saturday night. Containment efforts have been made more difficult by the region’s steep terrain.
author: Sejal Govindarao

title: Trump Says Reflecting Pool Will Likely Need to Be Drained
description: The pool has taken on clouds of algae after a hasty renovation. A three-time Olympian was charged with destroying government property after he says he touched one of the strands of blue paint peeling off the pool’s bottom.
author: Minho Kim

title: What We Know About the Los Angeles Warehouse Fire
description: Smoke spread across Los Angeles on Sunday, but the Fire Department said it was making progress in extinguishing the fire at the cold-storage warehouse. State and local officials have declared an emergency.
author: Pooja Salhotra and Maia Spoto

title: The Newsroom in D.C. Tries to Keep the Newsstand as a Shopping Destination
description: The Newsroom, overflowing with magazines and newspapers on mismatched shelves, has endured as a symbol of a different Washington.
author: Adam Sella and Alex Kent

title: 3 Hikers Die at Grand Canyon Amid ‘Dangerous’ High Temperatures
description: The hikers died at Grand Canyon National Park in separate episodes as temperatures peaked above 100 degrees, the authorities said.
author: Ishani Desai

title: ‘Agonizing for Everyone’: After IVF Mix-up, Birthing Couple Keeps Baby
description: One Florida couple discovered the baby they birthed did not come from their embryo, but they will retain custody in an agreement with the biological couple.
author: Billy Witz
